article.headline: Stuttgart slip to 3-1 loss at Gladbach in season restart
article.description: VfB Stuttgart, without their top scorer Serhou Guirassy, crashed to a 3-1 loss at Borussia Moenchengladbach on Sunday in their return to Bundesliga action after the winter break.
article.linkText: Stuttgart slip to 3-1 loss at Gladbach in season restart

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Robin Hack slots in the goal for Borussia Monchengladbach) 2024/01/14 ESHEET

article.story:
VfB Stuttgart, without their top scorer Serhou Guirassy, crashed to a 3-1 loss at Borussia Monchengladbach on Sunday in their return to Bundesliga action after the winter break.

Guinea international Guirassy, who has scored 17 times in the Bundesliga this season, is on duty at the Africa Cup of Nations, and without him Stuttgart were lacking their usual efficiency up front but are still third on 34 points, one ahead of RB Leipzig.

Gladbach's Robin Hack slotted in to give them the lead after just 21 seconds and he doubled their advantage 18 minutes later from a superb Alassane Plea through ball.

Stuttgart hit the post through Deniz Undav in the 32nd and had several more opportunities before Josha Vagnoman pulled one back with a powerful shot in the 56th.

With the visitors pushing for an equaliser, Gladbach struck on the break with Jordan slotting in on the rebound in stoppage time after Manu Kone's shot had bounced off the crossbar.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Borussia Mönchengladbach 1, VfB Stuttgart 0. Robin Hack (Borussia Mönchengladbach) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Rocco Reitz with a through ball.

Goal!  Borussia Mönchengladbach 2, VfB Stuttgart 0. Robin Hack (Borussia Mönchengladbach) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Alassane Pléa with a through ball.
Julian Weigl (Borussia Mönchengladbach) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Maximilian Mittelstädt (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2, VfB Stuttgart 0.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Anthony Rouault replaces Maximilian Mittelstädt.
